page: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-24xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. This x86_64 architecture system delivers 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached read-only small block transfers reaching 17118.15 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 521.53 GB/sec, both ranking in the top tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to its top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, database operations, and static web serving throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.metal-24xl offers high resource density and bare-metal execution, eliminating virtualization overhead. However, the complete absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, while the system demonstrates top-tier performance in CPU-based LLM inference, the lack of dedicated GPU accelerators limits its efficiency for large-scale deep learning training.

This server is configured for workloads requiring high-concurrency CPU processing and rapid memory access. Suitable applications include large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-bound machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-24xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server achieves top-tier status in both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, making it highly efficient for compute-dense tasks. The bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, though the absence of local storage requires network-attached alternatives. This configuration is optimized for high-concurrency workloads, including database management, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with a 96-core AMD EPYC processor, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-24xl is a 96-core AMD EPYC bare-metal server with 384 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-concurrency CPU workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and fast DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based inference
